riskMessages.low: "Prevention is better than cure." - Use strong passwords to keep threats at bay.
riskMessages.medium: "Better safe than sorry." - Don't let your guard down, use extra security measures to protect your information.
riskMessages.high: "Forewarned is forearmed." - Take immediate action to secure your accounts and review for suspicious activity.
riskMessages.unknown: "You never know what's around the corner." - Take precautionary measures, even if the risk isn't clear yet.

title4: Add or update account recovery details
desc1: Leaked passwords can be used to login to your other accounts if the passwords are the same or similar. By changing the password into a new and safe one, you take away the opportunity for hackers to easily access your data.
desc2: Activating 2FA will require you to use an additional code from SMS or authentication app when logging in. Even if your password is cracked, the hackers won't be able to login without the code.
desc3: Realizing that the information was leaked takes a long time. This gives time to hackers to access your accounts. Therefore it is recommended to check your accounts for suspicious activity.
desc4: By adding recovery information and keeping it updated, you ensure that in case of account theft, you could easily recover access.
desc5: Leaked email addresses are commonly targeted by phishing attacks. Be cautious when opening unknown emails, do not press on random url links.
